mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding employment and rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of 20-day forward returns against PAYEMS growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur less than 55% of the time when PAYEMS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.25%, the Fed signals an aggressively accommodative stance that compresses discount rates and elevates equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Combined moderation in CPI inflation (below 2% YoY) and steady payroll gains (above 150k per month) indicates a non-inflationary expansion that is ideal for equities, supporting positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon as risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails a bivariate test: Rank IC of the interaction (CPIAUCSL * PAYEMS) on 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the hypothesis underperforms a simple single-series benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 8%), declines in the unemployment rate signal a recovering labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During triggered periods, the correlation between UNRATE decreases and 60-day returns is not negative (i.e., falling UNRATE does not associate with positive returns), or the average return during such periods is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a weakening goods sector that often precedes broader economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the short-term (5 trading days) as risk aversion rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns in triggered instances are not negative on average (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate robust construction demand, which correlates with broader economic strength and rising home prices, supporting consumer wealth and confidence, and thus driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of 5-day returns on HOUST growth is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average return when HOUST increases is not significantly above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is above 2.5% annualized and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) is between 1% and 3%, the economy is in a 'Goldilocks' zone of solid growth without overheating, which is highly supportive for equities over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails a conditional test: 60-day returns during Goldilocks periods are not significantly higher than in non-Goldilocks periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the Rank IC of the interaction is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, the traditional negative relationship between policy rate increases and equities breaks down. Instead, a stable or slightly rising rate signals healthy normalization, while any rate hold is still accommodative, supporting positive forward returns.
failure_condition: In the ZLB regime, the average 20-day forward return is not positive, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and returns is positive (indicating rising rates are good) which is inconsistent with the hypothesis.
